steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage immediately explains that the Wi-Fi alternative "can give users a cheap way" to detect people through walls. This is the concrete benefit being described.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
Substitute each choice: a comparatively ______ alternative.

A) unmistakable — "unmistakable alternative" means easy to identify or obvious. The passage says nothing about recognizability; it contrasts cost. Eliminate.

B) inexpensive — "inexpensive alternative" directly echoes "cheap way" in the very next clause. Radar/sonar are "too costly" — the Wi-Fi method is cheap by comparison. Keep.

C) infrequent — "infrequent alternative" means rare or uncommon. Frequency is never discussed; this is about cost and accessibility. Eliminate.

D) impractical — "impractical alternative" means the Wi-Fi method doesn't work well. The passage explicitly says it can detect people and identify gestures, directly contradicting this. Eliminate.

B is correct.
